title: Anthropic Claude: GenAI Inference API (Claude Sonnet/Opus/Haiku)
description: State-of-the-art AI models (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, Haiku 4.5) via Anthropic API; strong safety focus; excellent reasoning and coding; competitive pricing; best-in-class for long-context analysis and code generation.

cost.costNotes: Competitive pricing with tiered model options. Claude Haiku is very cost-effective for high-volume tasks; Claude Sonnet balances quality and cost; Claude Opus is premium for complex reasoning. Implement token counting, prompt caching, and usage monitoring to control costs.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Pay-as-you-go token-based pricing creates variable costs. Min ($10): Small-scale usage with Claude Haiku (~50K tokens/day at $0.00025/1K input tokens). Max ($8,000): Heavy production usage with Claude Opus or Sonnet at scale (~1M tokens/day). Typical production chatbot with Claude Sonnet handling 100K tokens/day: $50-200/month. Anthropic's prompt caching feature can reduce costs significantly for repeated context patterns.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low-to-medium complexity integration. Clean REST API with official Python and TypeScript SDKs enables rapid implementation. Basic integration: 2-3 hours ($200-300 at $100/hr dev rate). Production-ready implementation requires: error handling, rate limiting, token counting, prompt engineering, and cost controls (additional 3-6 hours, $300-600). Total: 5-9 hours. Slightly lower than OpenAI due to simpler API surface (no image generation to handle). No specialized ML expertise required.
provenance.licenseCost: Pure consumption-based pricing model with no upfront license fees or subscriptions. Costs are entirely usage-based (pay-per-token). No ongoing free tier for production API use. Enterprise customers may negotiate volume discounts.

provides.reasoning: Core capability - this pattern provides generative AI inference services via Anthropic's API including Claude Sonnet, Opus, and Haiku models.
Specific capability provided by all Claude models for generating text content across various use cases and formats.
Conversational AI capability provided by Claude models for interactive dialogue and multi-turn conversations.
Anthropic Claude models support semantic understanding and can be used in conjunction with embedding workflows for similarity and retrieval tasks.
Claude supports structured tool use (Anthropic's term for function calling) which enables the model to invoke external tools and return structured data.
Claude models have strong vision and document reading capability, excelling at long-context document analysis, PDF processing, and structured data extraction.
Anthropic's API can be used as part of embedding generation pipelines for semantic understanding and retrieval-augmented generation workflows.

requires.reasoning: HTTP/REST client needed to communicate with Anthropic's API endpoints for all model interactions.
API keys must be securely stored and retrieved to authenticate with Anthropic services and prevent unauthorized access.
Token tracking is essential for cost management and staying within model context limits. Claude models have large context windows (up to 200K tokens) that require monitoring.
Consumption-based pricing requires tracking API usage costs to prevent budget overruns and optimize spending across model tiers.
Anthropic API has rate limits that need to be handled to prevent throttling and ensure smooth operation.
API calls may fail due to various reasons and need proper retry strategies and error recovery mechanisms.

availability.reason: Pattern for public SaaS with widespread adoption; documentation freely available.

supports_nfr.reason: Anthropic's managed API service provides up to 99.99% availability SLA for production workloads.
Anthropic API typical p95 latency for Claude Sonnet/Haiku inference is 500ms-2s; Haiku is faster, Opus is slower.
Anthropic API p99 latency for complex Claude requests typically ranges 1.5-8 seconds depending on token count, model tier, and load.
Anthropic's global infrastructure and automatic failover enables recovery within 1-5 minutes for API service disruptions.
Anthropic API enterprise tier supports up to 1000 requests/second (3.6M per hour) with rate limit quotas.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Anthropic API is cloud-based SaaS accessible from any cloud provider or on-premises environments via HTTP.
Anthropic provides official SDKs for Python and TypeScript with community libraries available for all major languages.
Anthropic API is HTTP-based and callable from any platform that can make REST requests over HTTPS.
Pattern specifically requires Anthropic as the backend provider.
Pattern is specifically designed for AI/ML inference using Anthropic Claude models and requires this capability.

primary_model.description: Primary text generation model. claude-opus-4-6: highest quality for complex reasoning, most expensive; claude-sonnet-4-6: best performance/cost balance for most use cases; claude-haiku-4-5: fastest and cheapest for high-volume simple tasks.

fallback_model.description: Fallback model if primary fails or rate-limited. claude-haiku-4-5: fast and cheap fallback; none: fail without fallback.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens per response. Higher values allow longer responses but increase cost and latency. Claude models support large context windows up to 200K input tokens.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0-1). Anthropic recommends 1.0 as default. Lower values (0-0.3): deterministic, factual responses; Higher values (0.7-1.0): more varied and creative outputs.

enable_streaming.description: Stream responses token-by-token for better UX in interactive applications. Disable for batch processing or when full response needed before processing.

timeout_seconds.description: API request timeout in seconds. Longer timeouts for complex prompts or long-context analysis; shorter for interactive applications.

retry_strategy.description: Retry strategy for rate limits and transient failures. exponential-backoff: recommended for production; fixed-delay: simpler but less efficient; none: fail immediately.

service_auth_method.description: Service credential method used to authenticate to the LLM provider API. Always api_key — stored in environment variable, never in code.

warn_nfr.reason: Anthropic API has consumption-based pricing. At <10 jobs/hour, consider whether a cheaper or self-hosted alternative is more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--anthropic: peak_jobs_per_hour is {actual} (<10). Low-volume usage may not justify Anthropic API costs; consider cheaper alternatives or local models.

warn_nfr.reason: Anthropic processes data on external servers. Sending PII to third-party APIs may violate compliance requirements (GDPR, HIPAA).
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--anthropic: spec indicates PII handling, but Anthropic API processes data externally which may violate data residency or compliance requirements.
